question: Is Ranked-Choice Voting the Best Method of Voting?
agent1_stance:
Your stance:
You support ranked-choice voting as the best method of voting.
You believe RCV eliminates costly runoff elections while ensuring winners have majority support and truly represent their communities.
You believe RCV allows votes to transfer if first choices don't win, enabling voters to support diverse candidates without fear of spoiling elections.
You believe RCV forces candidates to abandon negative campaign tactics since candidates need second and third-choice votes.
agent2_stance:
Your stance:
You oppose ranked-choice voting as the best method of voting.
You believe RCV can remove the candidate with the most first-place votes and may favor moderate candidates with only marginal support, while encouraging strategic voting schemes.
You believe ballot exhaustion occurs when ranked candidates are eliminated, effectively discarding votes, with exhaustion rates ranging from 9.6 percent to 27.1 percent.
You believe RCV is confusing, delays results significantly, and creates fraud vulnerabilities when multiple counting rounds are required, particularly at national scales.
